primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivable for designation as a hedged item in a hedge accounting relationship for foreign currency risk

primary_authority.reason: Defines what items qualify as hedged items and restricts designation to items with parties external to the reporting entity
Confirms hedge accounting applies only to foreign exchange differences arising between functional currencies of the foreign operation and parent entity

supporting_authority.reason: Provides exception for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items that result in exposure not fully eliminated on consolidation
Addresses treatment of exchange differences on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses presentation of gains/losses on financial liabilities designated at fair value but does not address hedged item eligibility
Addresses derecognition of financial liabilities, not hedged item eligibility for FX risk

treatment_families.family: Hedged item eligibility for intragroup foreign currency exposure

approaches.label: Hedge accounting permitted for intragroup FX exposure
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 paragraph 6 permits hedge accounting for intragroup monetary item FX risk when exposure is not fully eliminated on consolidation due to different functional currencies, and paragraph 589 confirms this applies to highly probable forecast intragroup transactions affecting consolidated profit or loss

approaches.label: Hedge accounting not permitted for intragroup dividend receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedged items to parties external to the reporting entity, and IFRIC 16 paragraph 10 confirms hedge accounting applies only to FX differences between functional currencies of the foreign operation and parent entity, which does not cover intragroup dividend receivables that lack external party exposure
